summary: Added project/toolchain support for the GC/1.1p1 Metrowerks compiler and used it to convert most of MetroTRK from assembly stubs to C, based on the OoT MetroTRK version. The PR removed large asm files for MetroTRK modules, added many C/header implementations, updated splits/symbols for new text/data/bss ownership, and marked most MetroTRK objects Matching while keeping the remaining weak spots non-matching.
what_changed: Pinned the compiler bundle in Docker and Nix to compilers_20250520.zip, replacing compilers_latest.zip in the Linux build Dockerfile and compilers_20240706.zip in .nix/mwcc.nix.
Added GC/1.1p1 compiler awareness: configure.py sets mw_version to GC/1.1p1 for TRK via a fix_trk path, and tools/project.py maps GC/1.1p1 to mwcc_233_159p1 for objdiff config generation.

Changed MetroTRK project object status from mostly NonMatching to mostly Matching, including mainloop, nubevent, nubinit, msg, msgbuf, serpoll, usr_put, dispatch, support, mutex_TRK, notify, flush_cache, mem_TRK, targimpl, mpc_7xx_603e, main_TRK, dolphin_trk_glue, and targcont.
Kept at least msghndlr.c and dolphin_trk.c marked NonMatching in configure.py, consistent with the PR body saying two files remained non-matching versus the OoT version.

Deleted large asm coverage for MetroTRK modules including targimpl.s, msgbuf.s, dolphin_trk_glue.s, mpc_7xx_603e.s, dispatch.s, nubinit.s, nubevent.s, serpoll.s, mainloop.s, flush_cache.s, main_TRK.s, msg.s, targcont.s, and mem_TRK_text.s.

Added or expanded C implementations and headers across MetroTRK, including targimpl.c, msghndlr.c, msgbuf.c, mpc_7xx_603e.c, support.c, dolphin_trk.c, dolphin_trk_glue.c, mem_TRK.c, dispatch.c, and many type/interface headers.
Updated GALE01 splits to assign MetroTRK .text, .init, .data, .rodata, and .bss ranges to C files; added new split entries for usr_put.c, support.c, mutex_TRK.c, and notify.c.

Renamed/reclassified several symbols in symbols.txt, for example replacing placeholder MTRK_NubInit_* bss names with gTRKEventQueue, gTRKMsgBufs, and gTRKFramingState, and replacing MTRK_Dispatch_80400788 with gTRKDispatchTableSize.
Added a small remaining bss allocation in asm/MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.s for MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 rather than fully eliminating every assembly fragment.
smart_moves: Introduced GC/1.1p1 as an explicit compiler version instead of relying on a generic/latest compiler bundle, making TRK matching reproducible across Docker, Nix, configure.py, and objdiff tooling.
Scoped the special compiler choice to TRK through a fix_trk path rather than globally changing all libraries to the new compiler.
Used the OoT MetroTRK version as a reference source, which is a strong tactic for runtime/vendor library code that likely shares upstream implementation patterns.
Split large monolithic asm coverage into logically named C modules and headers, improving future reviewability and type propagation.
Preserved nonmatching status for remaining problematic files instead of overclaiming matches; this makes the project state more honest and useful for later work.
Moved anonymous/placeholder bss symbols toward semantic global names where evidence was strong enough, improving decomp readability and split ownership.
decomp_lessons: Runtime/vendor libraries may require a compiler patch-level match, not just the broad GC compiler family; GC/1.1 and GC/1.1p1 were distinct enough to justify separate toolchain entries.
When adding a compiler variant, update every consumer: build containers, Nix derivation/hash, configure library metadata, and objdiff compiler maps.
For library decomp, external sibling projects or games can be high-value references, but remaining deltas should still be tracked as NonMatching.
Large assembly deletions should be paired with split and symbol ownership updates, especially for .bss ranges, otherwise matching C may not link into the same layout.
Replacing MTRK_* address-derived placeholders with gTRK* names helps communicate global state purpose once module identity is known.
Adding umbrella or module headers such as src/metrotrk.h and src/MetroTRK/*.h can be necessary before large-scale C conversion because cross-module TRK APIs are tightly coupled.
naming_conventions: MetroTRK public/global state uses gTRK* names where identified, such as gTRKDispatchTableSize, gTRKEventQueue, gTRKMsgBufs, gTRKBigEndian, gTRKFramingState, gTRKRestoreFlags, gTRKExceptionStatus, and gTRKStepStatus.
TRK functions retain upstream-style TRK prefixes, for example TRKInitializeDispatcher, TRKDispatchMessage, TRKDoReadMemory, TRKDoWriteMemory, TRKDoFlushCache, TRKDoContinue, and TRKDoStop.
MetroTRK headers use _METROTRK_* include guards, replacing address-based guards such as GALE01_3276A8 in dispatch.h.
Error/result typing moved from placeholder enum_t/UNK_RET patterns toward DSError and named constants such as kNoError and kDispatchError.
Message command handling uses MessageBuffer and msgcmd/msghndlr/msgbuf terminology rather than raw placeholder parameters.
Some unidentified or layout-only labels remain address-derived, such as MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 and MTRK_TargImpl_803B93F8, showing the PR did not invent semantic names without enough evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched MetroTRK with the GC/1.1p1 compiler via mwcc_233_159p1 and compiler bundle compilers_20250520.zip.
Deleted matched asm files once C replacements compiled to matching output, reducing reliance on hand-written assembly for dispatch, msgbuf, targimpl, glue, cache flush, and other TRK modules.
Left tiny or special assembly/data fragments where still needed, including added .bss in asm/MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.s and an Object(Matching, "MetroTRK/__exception.s") entry in configure.py.
Updated split ranges to account for extracted bss ownership, including gTRKEventQueue, gTRKMsgBufs, gTRKFramingState, and per-file MetroTRK bss sections.
Implemented dispatch as a typed callback table indexed by command, matching the prior assembly table while exposing TRKDoUnsupported/TRKDoConnect/etc. in C.

Used C-level struct/header reconstruction for PowerPC and TRK protocol surfaces, with new headers such as ppc_reg.h, m7xx_m603e_reg.h, ppc_targimpl.h, ppc_except.h, ppc_mem.h, msgcmd.h, msgbuf.h, and targimpl.h.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body states the intent clearly: add support for the 1.1p1 compiler and match most of MetroTRK using the OoT version, while acknowledging two files still non-matching versus that version.

The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased by 7320 bytes (+0.19%) and linked code increased by 736 bytes (+0.02%). The bot output also showed some MSL category shifts, likely related to the compiler bundle/toolchain update, but no human discussion explaining those shifts was included.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching MetroTRK/msghndlr.c or MetroTRK/dolphin_trk.c after PR 1494.
Search for other libraries using fix_trk or GC/1.1p1 to see whether the compiler variant remained TRK-only.
Audit whether MSL matched-code category shifts from the compiler bundle update were intentional or just reporting movement.
Look for future renames of MTRK_NubInit_804A50C8 or other remaining address-derived MetroTRK placeholders.
Compare this PR's OoT-derived MetroTRK headers against later header cleanup PRs for reusable type definitions.
